packageRules.description: PIN: @supabase/supabase-js is exact-pinned to 2.105.3 (Sprint 2 task 2.7 — closes auth-js GHSA-8r88-6cj9-9fh5). Bumps require manual ws-transport verification: versions >= 2.50 perform an eager WebSocket check at createClient() that fails on Node 20 unless the `ws` package is provided as transport. The current setup works (see backend/src/config/supabase.ts and docs/38-DEPENDENCY-RISK.md) but a future major bump might re-break the contract. Renovate must NOT auto-propose updates — manual upgrade only, following the test protocol in docs/38 §2.
Other Supabase packages (auth-helpers, postgrest-js, etc.) — group together. They evolve in coordination with supabase-js but are themselves not pinned.
Test toolchain — vitest + vite + esbuild evolve together and breaking changes in one require coordinated update of the others. Group all into one PR per cycle.
Vercel ecosystem (@vercel/node and friends). Group to keep PRs concise.
TypeScript + @types — coordinated upgrade keeps types in sync with compiler.
PDF tooling — pdf-lib + @pdf-lib/* together. Small ecosystem, low frequency, but tight coupling.
GitHub Actions runner versions (actions/checkout, actions/setup-node, etc.). Group all action upgrades into one PR.

Major version bumps ALWAYS require human review (no auto-merge regardless of dep type). Major bumps may have breaking API changes that even green CI cannot detect — runtime semantics, deprecations, behavioural changes in clinical-relevant libraries.

Production runtime dependencies (anything used by serverless functions or frontend at runtime) require human review for ANY update size. Includes pdf-lib, supabase-js (already pinned via separate rule), zod, @pdf-lib/fontkit. Clinical-grade software cannot auto-merge production deps without explicit review.

Auto-merge is currently DISABLED globally because the project does not enforce required status checks on main (founder's choice — direct push workflow). When branch protection is later upgraded with required CI, this rule can be relaxed for devDeps patch updates.
